senses.definition: used to refer to the practice of providing information only to those who absolutely require that specific information to perform their tasks or duties effectively
used to indicate one's complete knowledge or understanding of something

examples.x: The engineer understands the construction plans for this bridge back to front.
Our tour guide knows the history of this city back to front; you can ask her anything.
She's an expert in astrophysics and can explain the universe's mysteries back to front.
After working in the industry for years, she understands the regulations back to front.
Having grown up in the neighborhood, she knows the streets and alleys back to front.

word_list.word: to [know] {sth} like the back of {one's} hand

senses.definition: to be well-informed about or completely familiar with something

word_list.pronunciation: nňąo ä ňĆ…õsbňąiňź slňą√¶ É ňĆ…õstňĆiňźňąe…™t É …™nsňąa…™d ňąa ät

senses.definition: to have very good knowledge or understanding about someone or something

examples.x: After years of studying this topic, I know it inside out.
She's worked with this software extensively and knows it inside out.
Having grown up with him, I know my brother inside out.

senses.definition: to have easy, immediate access to something, often information or resources

examples.x: As an experienced pilot, Blake has emergency procedures at his fingertips in case of any issues during flight.
Once I had memorized all 50 states and their capitals, I'd had that information at my fingertips throughout childhood.
If she spent more time reviewing, the student would have the chemistry formulas at her fingertips for the final exam.

word_list.pronunciation: h√¶v …Ēňź…Ļ nňąo ä ňĆ…õstňĆiňźňąe…™t É dňĆa än pňą√¶t

senses.definition: to know something so perfectly that one can do it at anytime without having to focus or think

examples.x: She knows the piano piece down pat; she can play it flawlessly.
After years of practice, he has the presentation down pat and can deliver it without any hesitation.
The chef has the recipe down pat, and the dish always tastes amazing.
Once you've been in this job for a while, you'll have the procedures down pat.

senses.definition: in a state of being informed and knowledgeable about a particular topic or situation

senses.definition: a complete set of facts or details about how something is done or how it works

word_list.word: to [stand] on the shoulders of giants
word_list.pronunciation: stňą√¶nd …Ďňźn√į…ô  Éňąo äld…öz  Ćv d íňąa…™…ônts

senses.definition: to take advantage of the understanding and discoveries of influential figures who have been through the same or similar intellectual progress
some pieces of information or knowledge that may be useful or relevant at a later time

examples.x: She knows a thing or two about gardening, so ask her for advice on planting flowers.
After working in the industry for years, he's learned a thing or two about how to run a successful business.
Don't underestimate him; he may look young, but he's picked up a thing or two about negotiation.
I've been cooking for a long time, so I can teach you a thing or two about making a delicious pasta sauce.
Having traveled extensively, she's seen a thing or two and can share some interesting stories.

senses.definition: to be well-informed about a particular situation, to the point where one is able to determine the outcome

examples.x: She's been working in this company for years, so she really knows the score when it comes to office politics.
After researching the market for months, he knows the score and is ready to make an informed investment.
Don't try to deceive him; he's a seasoned detective and knows the score when it comes to solving crimes.
Having grown up in a tough neighborhood, she knows the score when it comes to street smarts.
In the world of finance, you have to know the score to make successful investments.

senses.definition: the knowledge or experience that is crucial for one to survive or succeed in urban environments

examples.x: She may not have a college degree, but she's got street smarts that help her succeed in any situation.
Having street smarts is essential when you're living in a big city.
Street smarts often come from life experiences and learning from mistakes.
You can't rely solely on book smarts; you need a good balance of street smarts too.
His street smarts helped him navigate through the challenges of the business world.
